Question
========
Which of the following statements about running code from an R script are correct?

Answerlist
----------
* You can run a single line of code by placing your cursor on that line
* You can run multiple lines by highlighting them
* You must always run all code in the script at once
* Scripts automatically run code as you type it
* The Control + Enter shortcut works for both individual lines and highlighted sections

Solution
========


Answerlist
----------
* True. The text states "you can now place your cursor at a line of your choosing and run that line individually."
* True. The text says "If you highlight all the lines of code, or just a subset of them, you can then run that highlighted section."
* False. The text explicitly mentions running lines "selectively or all together."
* False. Scripts do not automatically run code as you type.
* True. The same keyboard shortcut works for both scenarios as described in the text.

Meta-information
================
exname: running-code-options
extype: mchoice
exsolution: 11001
exshuffle: TRUE
